$schema_notes.purpose: Canonical building specification for Nanchan Temple Main Hall (南禅寺大殿) — primary data corpus for the Yingzao reconstruction pipeline. Every dimensional node carries a provenance tag and source citation. The Rule Engine derives geometry ONLY from values in this file plus the Yingzao Fashi ruleset; nothing renders without a source.

provenance_classes.measured: Direct instrument data: 3D laser scan point cloud (2mm density, ≤3mm global error) or caliper measurement (0.1mm precision), as published.
provenance_classes.reconstructed_design: Original 782 CE design value reconstructed by the survey team from measured data via cai-fen modular analysis (statistically derived, published as the building's intended dimensions). Treat as the authoritative build target.
provenance_classes.rule_derived: Value derived by OUR pipeline from Yingzao Fashi rules where no measured/reconstructed value exists. Must cite fascicle.
provenance_classes.conjecture: Flagged uncertain: altered by historical repairs or restored by modern intervention without firm evidence. Render allowed but MUST display in conjecture color.

$schema_notes.units: fen (分°) is the base module. 1 fen = 16.5 mm. 1 yingzao chi (营造尺) = 300 mm. mm values are derived: mm = fen × 16.5.

primary_source.citation: 张荣, 王一臻, 王麒, 李玉敏. 南禅寺大殿重建背景、材分营造制度分析及建筑像设空间布局研究. 建筑史学刊, 2022, 3(2): 5-23.
primary_source.method: Aerial oblique photogrammetry + 3D laser scanning + close-range digital photogrammetry; caliper measurement for bracket members.
primary_source.note: Dimension data are facts (not copyrightable); figures/drawings from this paper are NOT used as assets.

secondary_sources.QI1980: 祁英涛, 柴泽俊. 南禅寺大殿修复 / 修缮工程竣工技术报告 — record of the 1974–75 dismantling repair.
secondary_sources.YZFS: 李诫《营造法式》(1103). Public domain. Cited by fascicle (卷).

meta.location: 李家庄, 阳白乡, 东冶镇, 五台县, 忻州市, 山西省

date_built.evidence: Ink inscription on west four-rafter beam: 「因旧名峕大唐建中三年岁次壬戌月居戊申丙寅朔庚午日癸未时重修殿法显等谨志」

meta.status: Oldest surviving timber building in China; first batch National Key Cultural Relic (1961)
meta.structural_type: 三间×三间 厅堂式 (tingtang), 单檐歇山顶 (single-eave hip-gable), 彻上明造 (open ceiling, no pillars inside), 四架椽屋 (4-rafter frame)

major_repair.event: 1974–75 dismantling repair (落架大修) after 1966 Xingtai earthquake damage; design 祁英涛, execution 柴泽俊
major_repair.impact: Most timber members retained; plan/section dimensions unchanged; BUT eave rafters restored conjecturally and ridge-area members (camel humps, dwarf posts, large bearing blocks under ridge purlin) removed, keeping only the scissor braces (大叉手).

yingzao_chi_mm.note: Derived via distribution-consistency test (troughs at 276/286/301mm) cross-checked against cai-fen values and surviving Tang rulers (280–313mm); 275mm rejected. Same chi as Foguang East Hall — likely the Tang official standard.

fen_mm.note: From caliper survey of 57 hua-gong arms: single-cai arm width mean 163.41mm ≡ 10 fen; statistical analysis (SPSS, normal distribution, outliers removed) gives 16.3–16.5 mm/fen; 16.5 selected jointly with chi analysis.

cai_grade.value: 二等材 (Grade II of Yingzao Fashi's eight grades)
cai_grade.yzfs_ref: YZFS 卷四 材分八等
cai_grade.note: Foguang East Hall uses an extra-large 'special grade' (材厚7寸) exceeding Fashi Grade I — evidence that Song-era Fashi grades under-represent Tang building scale.

design_logic.note: Plan satisfies BOTH whole-hundred fen values (300/200) AND whole-or-half chi values (16.5/11) — dual-modulus design (整百分值 ∩ 整尺半尺).

plan.note: Perimeter columns only; column-free interior for the altar (厅堂式, 彻上明造).

pingzhu_height.note: Standard eave column (平柱), measured from plinth top of column 4-B (datum ±0). Controlled in chi, not fen.

corner_rise_shengqi.yzfs_ref: YZFS 卷五: 「至角则随间数生起，三间生高二寸」(would be 60mm)
corner_rise_shengqi.note: Measured corner rise 26.4–35.8mm → 2 fen (or 1.5 fen by square-column baseline). GENTLER than the Fashi rule — a documented deviation.

diameter_round.yzfs_ref: YZFS 用柱之制 (余屋 range)
diameter_round.note: Round columns; measured mean 384.16mm ≈ 23.3 fen → likely 23–24 fen design.

square_columns.note: Three square columns differ markedly from the round columns — likely NOT original 782 members (different period). Render with conjecture flag; their diameter/height inferences are unreliable.

bracket_sets_puzuo.configuration: 五铺作双杪偷心造 (5-puzuo, two hua-gong tiers, stolen-heart/no transverse arms on jumps), columns-top only — NO intercolumnar sets (无补间铺作)

ludou_capital_block.yzfs_ref: YZFS: 栌斗 32×32 fen, height 20 fen (耳8平4欹8)
ludou_capital_block.note: Smaller than Fashi spec in all dimensions — documented deviation.

hua_gong_outward_jumps.yzfs_ref: YZFS: 「每跳之长，心不过三十分°」 — jump 1 complies

hua_gong_inward_jumps.note: UNUSUAL: inward jumps LONGER than outward (vs. near-universal equal practice) and exceed Fashi's 30-fen cap — structural optimization to lengthen support under the four-rafter beams / ding beams.

zucai_strengthening.locations: 1st jump of column-top sets (front/rear/gable), 1st corner hua-gong, 2nd jump of central-bay column-top sets
zucai_strengthening.source: ZHANG2022 (57-sample caliper study; ANOVA F=98.1 >> Fcrit, p=8.18e-11)
zucai_strengthening.note: Load-bearing hua-gong widened by 1 fen — keystone evidence that the builders adapted the modular system for structural mechanics. Predates similar Ming/Qing practice by centuries.

transverse_arms.note: All longer than Foguang's (62/58/104); ling-gong NOT shorter than nidao-gong, breaking the usual early pattern.

puzuo_total_height.note: Ludou underside → liaoyan purlin top. Each tier = full zucai 21 fen; ludou lift = 11 fen (vs. typical 12).

details.note: Half ingot-tenons (银锭榫) carved at arm heads to retain small blocks; small camel humps + min-boards + san-dou atop 2nd column-top tie supporting the ox-spine tie (牛脊枋) — rare in later buildings.

roof_frame.type: 四架椽屋 (4-rafter), 单檐歇山 (hip-gable), 彻上明造

purlin_spacing.sequence: 牛脊枋 → 平槫 → 脊槫, equal 150-fen intervals (total depth 600 fen / 4 rafters)

liaoyan_to_niuji_horizontal.note: = sum of the two outward bracket jumps (27+20).

total_rise_juwu.yzfs_ref: YZFS 举屋之法: span/3 rise would be ~231 fen; 折屋 rule would give 13-fen depression
total_rise_juwu.note: Measured ratio 1:2.67 — far gentler than Fashi's 1:3 rule and gentler than Foguang (1:2.23), consistent with early-Tang gentle roofs. FLAGGED conjecture because the 1974–75 repair removed ridge-area members (驼峰/短柱/大斗), possibly altering rise. Mid-purlin depression measured 10 fen (vs. 13 by rule).

vertical_control_logic.note: Total height controlled by three clean numbers: column 13 chi + puzuo 95 fen + roof rise 130 fen.

ridge_structure.note: Camel humps, dwarf posts, and bearing blocks under ridge purlin were REMOVED in the 1974–75 repair. Original ridge configuration uncertain — prime candidate for the conjecture layer.

eave_projection.note: Pre-repair rafters were short (clearly not Tang appearance); 2340mm is the 1974–75 restoration estimate from platform/column-height ratios. Explicitly unusable as design evidence — MUST render in conjecture color.

altar_clearances.note: U-shaped altar fills the hall, leaving a circumambulation ring (右绕礼佛).

key_heights_chi.note: Statue heights follow whole/half chi (NOT fen) — corroborates the 300mm chi. Main Buddha ≈ 2× human height per《祇洹寺图经》.

altar_and_statues.narrative_hook: Earliest extant 'New-Style Manjusri' (新样文殊) group in China — the lion-tamer is the King of Khotan in Tang military dress, tied to post-An Lushan Manjusri state cult under Emperor Daizong.

sightline_design.name: 高三距五 (rise-3 : run-5) hypothesis
stations: Platform edge → full bracket sets visible at exactly 31° to liaoyan purlin underside
Doorway → full mandorla at 31°; eye level meets right Guardian King's eyes
Altar outer edge (6 chi inside door) → Buddha's crown at 31°; eye level meets attendant bodhisattva's eyes
Altar inner edge (+2.5 chi) → Buddha's EYES at 31°; eye level meets Manjusri/Samantabhadra crowns
sightline_design.source: ZHANG2022 (建 on 傅熹年 30° analysis)
sightline_design.note: Identical scheme verified at Foguang East Hall. THE backbone for the walkthrough narrative mode — camera path should reproduce these four stations.

verifier_targets.note: Machine-checkable assertions the Vision Verifier must confirm on rendered output.

checks.assert: pingzhu height = 13 chi; corner columns +2 fen rise
puzuo = 5-puzuo, 2 hua-gong tiers, column-top only, no intercolumnar sets
outward jumps 27+20 fen; liaoyan offset from niuji = 47 fen
puzuo stack height = 95 fen from ludou underside to liaoyan top
purlin intervals = 150 fen × 4; liaoyan span = 694 fen
roof rise ≈ 130 fen (ratio ≈ 1:2.67), GENTLER than Fashi 1:3 — verifier must NOT 'correct' toward the Fashi rule; measured reality wins
hip-gable roof form; open interior, zero interior columns
provenance audit: every rendered component carries a class; eave projection + ridge members + square columns colored conjecture
load-bearing hua-gong rendered 11 fen wide vs 10 fen elsewhere (the zucai story must be visible in close-up)

fashi_deviations_index.note: Components where the 782 building disagrees with the 1103 Fashi — the provenance layer's exhibit list. Pipeline rule: measured reality overrides the rulebook; deviations are annotated, never 'fixed'.
items: corner rise 2 fen, gentler than 「三间生高二寸」
